title: Addressee gate ahead of the yield decision (generic)
approach: Insert an addressee-classification step between VAD/ASR onset detection and the turn-taking controller's yield decision. Speech energy on the caller channel is not, by itself, evidence the speech was directed at the agent -- it may be side conversation, a phone call in the background, or speech to a third party in the room. The gate evaluates whether detected speech is addressed to the agent using signals independent of simple audio energy (for example: acoustic proximity/orientation cues, or an explicit push-to-talk / wake-phrase channel where the stack has one), and only speech classified as addressed is forwarded to the controller as a candidate floor-taking or interruption event.
integration_points: sits between the VAD/ASR event stream and the turn-taking controller's yield decision

requires an addressee signal the controller does not otherwise have (proximity/orientation, explicit push-to-talk, or an equivalent addressed-speech channel) -- this is the part current energy-only VAD pipelines are missing

emits an addressed_to_agent classification (true | false | unknown) per caller utterance onset; only true is forwarded as a floor-taking event, unknown degrades to the existing energy-only behavior rather than guessing
